description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: rspec, configtest, and publish, using a global ruby:2.6 Docker image and caching the vendor directory. Define a global before_script that starts ssh-agent, adds the decoded $CI_PRIVATE_KEY, creates an ~/.ssh config disabling strict host key checking, prints the Ruby version, installs bundler without documentation, and runs bundle install with parallel jobs into vendor. Add an rspec job in the rspec stage that inherits the global before_script, runs bundle exec rspec -f d, and is only executed when the CI_PRIVATE_KEY variable is present. Add a config-test job in the configtest stage that overrides the before_script to copy test configs to /usr/share/logstash/pipeline/, patch a gemspec with sed, install Logstash plugins in development mode, uses the docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:5.6.5 image, and runs ./tests/bin/logstash-config.sh. Add a push-cookbook job in the publish stage that also inherits the global before_script, runs only on the master branch when OPS_CLONE_USERNAME and OPS_CLONE_PASSWORD variables are set, clones the cookbook-publisher repository via HTTPS with those credentials into /tmp/cookbook-publisher, copies publisher.rb, and executes ruby publisher.rb.
